pattern.rationale: src/features/<feature>/ slice with co-located view (ThresholdCounter.tsx), hook (useThresholdCounter.ts), test (ThresholdCounter.test.tsx) and a barrel (index.ts); main.tsx + App.tsx compose above. Matches .agents/project/ARCHITECTURE.md.

snapshot.note: Graph-derived metrics (god nodes, communities, fan-in/out, PageRank) not computed: graphify was not run (noGraphify). Also structurally trivial at 6 modules / 1 feature.

checks.expectation: The dependency graph is acyclic; cycles detected by graphify.
checks.gap: Cycle detection requires the knowledge graph, which was not built. Static read shows a strict import tree with no back-edge; a cycle is structurally impossible across 6 modules, but this is asserted, not graph-proven.
checks.remediation: Run /pre-audit-setup to build graphify-out/graph.json, then re-run for a confirmed acyclic result.

checks.expectation: Each feature under src/features/ exposes a barrel (index.ts) and external imports go through it.
checks.gap: Holds, but for exactly one feature with one external consumer (App.tsx imports the barrel, not a deep path). Discipline at scale is unobserved.
checks.remediation: None now. Re-assess once a second feature exists; that is the first real test of feature isolation.

checks.expectation: Relative imports go up at most two levels.
checks.gap: None. grep for ../../../ across src/ returns zero matches; all intra-feature imports are single-level siblings.

checks.expectation: Sibling features import each other only through public entry points (feature-folders pattern).
checks.gap: Structural prerequisite absent: only one feature exists, so there are no sibling boundaries to cross. The rule is documented in .agents/project/ARCHITECTURE.md ('Features are islands') but is untested.
checks.remediation: None now. The invariant becomes testable when a second feature lands.

checks.expectation: Internal packages import only from each other's published entry points (monorepos only).
checks.gap: Not a monorepo: pnpm-workspace.yaml carries only allowBuilds; no packages/ directory or workspace globs. Skipped per SKILL.md.

checks.expectation: No module has fan-in greater than 30.
checks.gap: Fan-in/PageRank are graph-derived and were not computed. With 6 modules the maximum possible fan-in is trivially small, so a god module cannot exist at this size.
checks.remediation: Re-run after /pre-audit-setup for a measured fan-in distribution.

checks.expectation: No React component is rendered by more than 25 parents.
checks.gap: Render-graph not built. ThresholdCounter has exactly one rendering parent (App.tsx), structurally far below threshold.
None. Largest TS/TSX source is 52 lines; index.css (152) is not a TS source.

checks.expectation: No component imports more than 15 modules.
checks.gap: Per-component import graph not built. Static read: ThresholdCounter.tsx imports 1 module, App.tsx imports 1 — both trivially under budget.

checks.expectation: Every source file is reachable from at least one entry point.
checks.gap: Reachability is a graph traversal and was not run. Static read: every src/ module is reachable from main.tsx; no orphans by inspection.
checks.remediation: Re-run after /pre-audit-setup to confirm against the graph.

checks.gap: None. No state library in dependencies; state is React-local only (useState/useMemo/useCallback). Single coherent strategy.
checks.remediation: None. When global state is needed, pick one library and record it as an ADR per .agents/project/ARCHITECTURE.md.

checks.expectation: Server state lives in a designated data-fetching layer, not component useEffect + fetch.
checks.gap: Structural prerequisite absent: no data fetching anywhere (grep for fetch(/axios/XMLHttpRequest in src/ returns zero). ARCHITECTURE.md deliberately defers a data layer.
checks.remediation: N/A now. When a data layer is added, give it its own boundary (e.g. src/shared/api) with a public surface.

checks.expectation: No top-level 'let' exports holding mutable state.
checks.gap: None. grep for 'export let' in src/ returns zero; module-level exports are const constants and pure functions/types.

checks.expectation: Components do not read/write localStorage, sessionStorage, document.cookie, or window.* directly.
checks.gap: None. grep across src/ returns zero matches. The one DOM access (document.getElementById('root')) is in main.tsx, the composition root, and fails loudly if #root is missing.

checks.expectation: 'use client' used deliberately (Next.js App Router only).
checks.gap: Not Next.js App Router (Vite-React). Directive does not apply. Skipped per SKILL.md.

checks.expectation: Files match the dominant naming pattern for their kind.
checks.gap: None. Folder kebab-case, component PascalCase, hook useCamelCase same-name, test co-located <Name>.test.tsx, constants SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, types PascalCase. All match CONVENTIONS.md.

checks.expectation: Directories of the same kind follow the same organisational convention.
checks.gap: The single feature folder matches the canonical four-file shape in ARCHITECTURE.md. No siblings to diverge yet, so consistency is satisfied trivially.

checks.expectation: If barrels are used in any feature folder, they are used in every feature folder.
checks.gap: The one feature has a barrel; no second feature to be inconsistent with. Convention codified in ARCHITECTURE.md rule 3 and CONVENTIONS.md.

checks.expectation: Each feature's index.ts has a documenting comment describing its public surface, or the folder has a README.md (soft check).
checks.gap: index.ts is a bare one-line re-export with no doc comment, and the folder has no README.md. The component and hook are well-documented, but the public surface (the barrel) itself is not.
checks.remediation: Add a 1-3 line doc comment to index.ts naming what the feature exposes and what stays private. Sets the template every future feature copies.
